data.title: Singapore's Straits Trading to sell South Korea logistics asset for about $296 million
data.description:
Singapore-listed Straits Trading Company said on Friday it had agreed to sell a logistics property in South Korea through its joint venture for around S$378 million ($296 million).

The disposal will be carried out through Sky Logis Private Real Estate Investment, which owns the Arenas Yeongjong Logistics Center in Incheon, South Korea.

The buyer is a major South Korean financial institution acting as trustee of a private trust, the company said.

Straits Trading has an effective interest of 47.3% in Sky Logis.

Completion of the sale is scheduled for January 29 subject to customary conditions, including regulatory clearances and the fulfilment of representations and warranties by both parties, the company said.

($1 = 1.2778 Singapore dollars)

data.title: US consumer sentiment improves in January
data.description:
U.S. consumer sentiment improved across the board in January, though concerns about high prices and the labor market lingered, a survey showed on Friday.

The University of Michigan's Surveys of Consumers said its Consumer Sentiment Index increased to a final reading of 56.4 this month, from an earlier estimate of 54.0. The index was at 52.9 in December. Economists polled by Reuters had forecast the index would be unrevised from the preliminary estimate.

"While the overall improvement was small, it was broad based, seen across the income distribution, educational attainment, older and younger consumers, and Republicans and Democrats alike," Joanne Hsu, the director of the Surveys of Consumers, said in a statement. "However, national sentiment remains more than 20% below a year ago, as consumers continue to report pressures on their purchasing power stemming from high prices and the prospect of weakening labor markets."

The survey's measure of consumer expectations for inflation over the next year slipped to 4.0%, the lowest reading since January 2025, from an earlier estimate of 4.2%. Consumers' expectations for inflation over the next five years dipped to 3.3% from a preliminary estimate of 3.4%. Long-term inflation expectations edged up from 3.2% last month.

data.title: South African rand stutters around 16/$ mark while traders eye rate decision next week
data.description:
The South African rand slipped on Friday but hovered near the 16-per-dollar level as traders looked ahead to next week's interest rate decision for signals on the central bank's policy stance.

At 1449 GMT, the rand traded at 16.1825 per dollar  , roughly down 0.3% on Thursday's close but still near its strongest level since June 2022.

The currency has been supported by record-high gold prices, improved fiscal indicators, a credible central bank and sustained dollar weakness, said Citadel Global director Bianca Botes in a note.

The risk-sensitive rand, which often tracks major global drivers such as U.S. policy, has gained more than 2% against the dollar since the start of 2026.

"A test of 16.00 per dollar appears plausible, should gold hold above $4,800 an ounce and dollar weakness persist," Botes said.

The dollar  was last flat against a basket of currencies, while gold inched toward another record high of $5,000 an ounce, supported by geopolitical uncertainty.

Botes cautioned that South Africa's weak growth outlook, uncertainty over its continued inclusion in the African Growth and Opportunity Act, reliance on a potentially overvalued gold price and the rand's inherent volatility all suggest the currency's recent gains may prove fragile.

The South African Reserve Bank will deliver its monetary policy decision next week, after cutting its main lending rate by 25 basis points in November.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index  was up 0.7%.

South Africa's benchmark 2035 government bond  (ZAR2035=) firmed, with the yield falling 9 basis points to 8.165%.

data.title: iRobot to Launch U.S. Data Protection Unit As Part of Chapter 11 Restructuring
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

iRobot transferred all of its equity to Shenzhen Picea Robotics and said it will launch a U.S. subsidiary to protect U.S. consumer data as it emerges from the chapter 11 bankruptcy process

The maker of Roombas said Friday that it would launch the U.S unit, iRobot Safe, as one of several measures to protect U.S. and global consumer data included in the court-supervised restructuring plan.

The unit will have an independent board composed of U.S. citizens, along with a chief executive officer and an independent U.S.-based data security officer.

"This structure and other controls are intended to provide regulators, consumers, and partners with confidence that iRobot's data governance framework is designed to protect U.S. and other global consumer data," the company said.

With the end of the bankruptcy process, iRobot is now a private company fully owned by Picea, it said. The company said it will maintain its Bedford, Mass. headquarters and continue to function as a global consumer robotics company.

"Building on iRobot's strong foundation, I look forward to working with the Picea leadership team as we enter our next chapter with renewed momentum," iRobot Chief Executive Officer Gary Cohen said. "My conviction in iRobot has never been stronger, and together with Picea, we are focused on delivering reliable, high-quality products, supporting our customers, protecting consumer data, and operating with discipline as we move forward."

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Strong Nov Retail Sales in Canada May Reflect Black Friday Sales — Market Talk
data.description: A strong rebound in Canadian retail sales in November likely mostly reflects a temporary boost from Black Friday deals rather than a more lasting pickup in consumer demand, given an estimate 0.5% fall on-month in December sales, Capital Economics' Bradley Saunders says. The economist still believes the worst is behind Canada in terms of consumer spending, as earlier interest rate cuts feed through and consumer confidence continues the climb off its recent bottom. But the recovery looks set be gradual, Saunders says. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbM.Stewart)

data.title: TikTok Finalizes US Investor Deal to Avoid Nationwide Ban
data.description:
Oracle and a group of American investors have finalized a long-anticipated transaction that could preserve TikTok's operations in the US, ending a multi-year regulatory standoff tied to national security concerns. The agreement establishes a new US-based TikTok entity led by Oracle, Silver Lake Management and Abu Dhabi-backed MGX, with TikTok CEO Shou Chew retaining global oversight of the platform while joining the board of the US venture. Adam Presser, previously responsible for operations, trust and safety at TikTok, has been appointed CEO of the American business, signaling a governance reset designed to align the platform more closely with US regulatory expectations.

The deal resolves legislation passed by Congress in 2024 that required ByteDance to divest TikTok's US operations or face a nationwide ban, citing risks around data security and potential foreign influence. TikTok has consistently maintained that US user data has not been misused and that the platform has not served as a vehicle for state-directed narratives. US President Donald Trump publicly welcomed the agreement, crediting TikTok with playing a role in his 2024 election victory and thanking Chinese President Xi Jinping for approving the transaction. While the final valuation of the US entity has not been formally disclosed, Vice President JD Vance has referenced a figure of about $14 billion, compared with prior external estimates that placed TikTok's US business between $35 billion and $50 billion.

Under the ownership structure announced by the White House last September, new US investors will hold 50% of the TikTok US entity, with existing ByteDance investors owning 30.1% and ByteDance retaining a 19.9% stake. The new company will oversee content moderation and US user data protection, operating under a seven-member, majority-American board, with Oracle serving as a compliance and security partner. ByteDance is expected to lease a version of its recommendation algorithm to the US entity, which would be retrained on American user data, while potentially continuing to control key business lines such as advertising and TikTok Shop. Some critics have argued the arrangement may not fully satisfy the 2024 national security law's intent, leaving open the possibility of future legal challenges.

data.title: Life360 climbs on preliminary annual results
data.description:
** U.S.-listed shares of family-tracking app provider Life360 jump 28.2% to $70.19

** Co announces preliminary annual results above Street view

** Stock on track for biggest one-day jump since its U.S. debut in July 2024

** Co says it expects FY25 revenue between $486 and $489 mln, about 31%-32% above last year, and higher than analysts' estimate of $481.65 mln - data compiled by LSEG

** Preliminary results show LIF had 95.8 mln monthly active users in Q4, with 576,000 user additions in 2025, marking co's strongest annual subscriber growth

** CEO Lauren Antonoff says newly acquired users are converting to paid subscriptions at record rates

** Co expects about 20% growth in monthly active users in 2026

** Including session's move, LIF stock up 44% over the past year

data.title: Canadian Retail Trade Remains Choppy — Market Talk
data.description: Canadian retail sales continue to oscillate, as they have for the past year, rebounding in November only to fall back in December, notes Desjardins' Oskar Stone. Retail receipts rose 1.3% on-month in November, roughly in line with expectations, while the flash estimate points to a 0.5% decline for December. Stone's tracking for 4Q GDP now points to a modest 0.25% growth rate, which the economist says looks difficult for the Bank of Canada to suggest monetary policy is stimulative. Desjardins estimates Canadian retail sales volumes fell 1.6% annualized in 4Q. (robb.stewart@wsj.com; @RobbM.Stewart)

data.title: Materion Corp drops after SEC filing shows quality issue-related charges
data.description:
** Shares of alloys and chemicals maker Materion Corp fall 7.83% to $137.34

** In Q4, a large precision clad strip customer identified a quality issue with Materion-produced material used in its manufacturing process

** MTRN expects to record a charge of about $20 million to $25 million due to the quality issue

** Charge includes a quality claim, material scrap expenses and temporary plant idling costs - MTRN

** With restricted clad strip sales in Q4, co sees adjusted EPS of $1.50 to $1.55 for the quarter vs. analysts' estimates of $1.60 - data compiled by LSEG

** MTRN rose 25.73% in 2025
